data.content:
Implementation reviewed against acceptance criteria.

Acceptance check:
1. ✓ secrets/recipients.signed.toml policy file (TOML subset, hand-rolled parser, no third-party dep). Validates M ≥ 1, M ≤ N, no duplicates, recipient format.
2. ✓ recipient add requires signatures (3-phase prepare/sign/commit) when policy present.
3. ✓ --bootstrap valid only at <2 recipients; rejected at 2+ with clear message.
4. ✓ recipient list prints prefix + thumbprint + recipient.
5. ✓ Audit log Signers field captures signing thumbprints; Bootstrap field flags chicken-and-egg adds.
6. ✓ Replay-resistant: canonical message includes <app>:<env>:<new>:<bundle-sha>:<nonce>, stale SHA rejected.
7. ✓ Unforgeable: each operator's .sig is a re-encryption of the canonical message after decrypting their per-operator challenge with their private key.

All four verification commands pass: go build, go test, make lint, go test -tags integration.

End-to-end smoke verified by hand against real age binary: bootstrap (1->2), policy file installed, prepare creates challenges, two operators sign, commit succeeds, new recipient can decrypt the bundle. Quorum-short flow returns '1/2 signatures present (need from bob or carol)'.

File sizes: all <500 lines per file, all functions <100 lines (lll/funlen pass).

No third-party deps added. Bespoke TOML subset parser handles the small surface area we accept.

Commit: 3380eba8bcb54efc80001d1cd4d68c2dfb6a2703
Branch: claude/elastic-clarke-9c0ed3
